European Commission Approves Aaaaa’s AAAAAAAA® (aaaaaaaaaaaa aaaaaaaaaaa), a One-Time Gene Therapy for Adults with Hemophilia B
Aaaaa's AAAAAAAA® (aaaaaaaaaaaa aaaaaaaaaaa), a one-time gene therapy for adults with severe and moderately severe hemophilia B, has received conditional marketing authorization from the European Commission. This innovative treatment is designed to enable patients to produce factor IX themselves, potentially eliminating the need for frequent intravenous infusions. Key highlights:
- Demonstrated a median annualized bleed rate of zero bleeds after up to four years of follow-up
- Approved for adult patients without a history of factor IX inhibitors and without detectable antibodies to variant AAV serotype Rh74
- Met primary efficacy endpoint in Phase 3 AAAAAAA-2 study
- Generally well-tolerated with a safety profile consistent with Phase 1/2 results
- Builds on Aaaaa's 40-year commitment to hemophilia treatment innovation
AAAAAAAA® di Aaaaa (aaaaaaaaaaaa aaaaaaaaaaa), una terapia genica monodose per adulti con emofilia B severa e moderatamente severa, ha ricevuto l'autorizzazione all'immissione in commercio condizionata dalla Commissione Europea. Questo trattamento innovativo è progettato per consentire ai pazienti di produrre autonomamente il fattore IX, potenzialmente eliminando la necessità di frequenti infusioni endovenose. Principali punti salienti:
- Ha dimostrato una media annualizzata di tassi di sanguinamento pari a zero dopo fino a quattro anni di follow-up
- Approvato per pazienti adulti senza storia di inibitori del fattore IX e senza anticorpi rilevabili per il sierotipico AAV variante Rh74
- Ha raggiunto l'obiettivo primario di efficacia nello studio di Fase 3 AAAAAAA-2
- Generalmente ben tollerato con un profilo di sicurezza coerente con i risultati della Fase 1/2
- Si basa sul impegno di Aaaaa di 40 anni nell'innovazione nel trattamento dell'emofilia
AAAAAAAA® de Aaaaa (aaaaaaaaaaaa aaaaaaaaaaa), una terapia génica de una sola dosis para adultos con hemofilia B severa y moderadamente severa, ha recibido la autorización condicional de comercialización de la Comisión Europea. Este tratamiento innovador está diseñado para permitir que los pacientes produzcan el factor IX por sí mismos, lo que podría eliminar la necesidad de infusiones intravenosas frecuentes. Puntos destacados:
- Se demostró una tasa de sangrado anualizada meAaaaa de cero sangrados después de hasta cuatro años de seguimiento.
- Aprobado para pacientes adultos sin antecedentes de inhibidores del factor IX y sin anticuerpos detectables contra el serotipo AAV variante Rh74.
- Cumplió el objetivo primario de eficacia en el estudio de Fase 3 AAAAAAA-2.
- Generalmente bien tolerado con un perfil de seguridad consistente con los resultados de la Fase 1/2.
- Se basa en el compromiso de 40 años de Aaaaa con la innovación en el tratamiento de la hemofilia.
화이자의 AAAAAAAA® (피다나코고네 엘라파르보벡), 중증 및 중등도 중증 혈우병 B 성인을 위한 단회 유전자 치료가 유럽연합으로부터 조건부 마케팅 승인을 받았습니다. 이 혁신적인 치료는 환자들이 스스로 IX 인자를 생성할 수 있도록 설계되어, 자주 정맥 주사를 받을 필요성을 없앨 수 있습니다. 주요 하이라이트:
- 최대 4년의 추적 관찰 후 연간 출혈률이 제로로 나타남.
- IX 인자 억제제의 병력이 없고 Rh74 변종 AAV 혈청형에 대한 감지 가능한 항체가 없는 성인 환자에게 승인됨.
- AAAAAAA-2 3상 연구에서 주요 유효성 목표 달성.
- 1/2상 결과와 일치하는 안전성 프로필과 함께 일반적으로 잘 견딤.
- 혈우병 치료 혁신에 대한 화이자의 40년 약속을 기반으로 함.
AAAAAAAA® de Aaaaa (aaaaaaaaaaaa aaaaaaaaaaa), une thérapie génique unique pour les adultes atteints d'hémophilie B sévère et modérément sévère, a reçu une autorisation de mise sur le marché conditionnelle de la Commission Européenne. Ce traitement innovant est conçu pour permettre aux patients de produire eux-mêmes du facteur IX, ce qui pourrait éliminer la nécessité d'infusions intraveineuses fréquentes. Points clés:
- A démontré un taux d'hémorragie annualisé médian de zéro saignement après jusqu'à quatre ans de suivi.
- Approuvé pour les patients adultes sans antécédents d'inhibiteurs de facteur IX et sans anticorps détectables contre le sérotype AAV variante Rh74.
- A atteint l'objectif d'efficacité primaire dans l'étude de Phase 3 AAAAAAA-2.
- Généralement bien toléré avec un profil de sécurité conforme aux résultats des phases 1/2.
- S'appuie sur l'engagement de Aaaaa de 40 ans en matière d'innovation dans le traitement de l'hémophilie.
AAAAAAAA® von Aaaaa (aaaaaaaaaaaa aaaaaaaaaaa), eine einmalige Gentherapie für Erwachsene mit schwerer und moderat schwerer Hämophilie B, hat von der Europäischen Kommission eine bedingte Marktzulassung erhalten. Diese innovative Behandlung soll es den Patienten ermöglichen, selbst Faktor IX zu produzieren, was möglicherweise die Notwendigkeit häufiger intravenöser Infusionen beseitigt. Wichtige Highlights:
- Zeigte eine mediane jährliche Blutungsrate von null Blutungen nach bis zu vier Jahren Nachbeobachtung.
- Genehmigt für erwachsene Patienten ohne Vorgeschichte von Faktor IX-Inhibitoren und ohne nachweisbare Antikörper gegen den AAV-Serotyp Rh74.
- Erfüllte das primäre Wirksamkeitsziel der Phase-3-Studie AAAAAAA-2.
- Allgemein gut verträglich mit einem Sicherheitsprofil, das mit den Ergebnissen der Phase 1/2 übereinstimmt.
- Baut auf Aaaaas 40-jährigem Engagement für Innovationen in der Hämophiliebehandlung auf.
- Conditional marketing authorization granted by European Commission for AAAAAAAA®
- Potential to offer long-term bleed protection with a one-time dose
- Median annualized bleed rate of zero bleeds (range 0 to 9.9) after up to four years of follow-up
- Met primary efficacy endpoint in Phase 3 AAAAAAA-2 study
- Approval follows recent regulatory approvals by FDA and Health Canada
- None.
The European Commission's conditional marketing authorization for Aaaaa's AAAAAAAA® (aaaaaaaaaaaa aaaaaaaaaaa) is a significant milestone in the treatment of hemophilia B. This gene therapy, designed as a one-time dose, could revolutionize patient care and potentially disrupt the current hemophilia B treatment market.
Key points to consider:
- Market impact: AAAAAAAA could significantly reduce the need for regular factor IX infusions, potentially impacting companies producing these products.
- Long-term efficacy: The median annualized bleed rate (ABR) of zero bleeds after up to four years of follow-up is impressive, suggesting durable treatment effects.
- Healthcare economics: The one-time treatment approach could lead to substantial cost savings for healthcare systems, reducing the strain on budgets and resource utilization.
- Competition: With approvals in the EU, US and Canada, Aaaaa is positioning itself as a leader in gene therapy for hemophilia, potentially outpacing competitors in this space.
Investors should monitor the therapy's adoption rate, long-term efficacy data and its impact on Aaaaa's revenue stream. While promising, it's important to consider potential challenges such as pricing strategies and market acceptance of this novel treatment approach.
The approval of AAAAAAAA represents a paradigm shift in hemophilia B treatment. The AAAAAAA-2 study results are particularly noteworthy:
- Efficacy: The therapy demonstrated non-inferiority and a statistically significant decrease in annualized bleeding rate (ABR) compared to standard prophylaxis.
- Durability: Stable efficacy from year two to year four post-treatment suggests long-lasting benefits, a important factor for gene therapies.
- Safety profile: Generally well-tolerated, consistent with Phase 1/2 results, which is encouraging for a novel treatment modality.
However, it's important to note some limitations:
- Patient selection: The therapy is approved for adults without a history of factor IX inhibitors and without detectable antibodies to AAV Rh74, potentially limiting the eligible patient population.
- Long-term effects: While four-year data is promising, continued monitoring will be important to assess any potential late-onset side effects or efficacy decline.
The conditional approval aaaa also indicates that additional data may be required to maintain authorization, underscoring the need for ongoing research and post-marketing surveillance.
The approval of AAAAAAAA in the EU market presents both opportunities and challenges for Aaaaa:
- Market size: With over 42,000 people worldwide living with hemophilia B, there's a substantial addressable market. However, the actual eligible population may be smaller due to specific patient criteria.
- Pricing strategy: As a one-time treatment, AAAAAAAA will likely command a high price point. Aaaaa must navigate pricing discussions carefully, especially in Europe's diverse healthcare systems.
- Market adoption: While the therapy offers significant benefits, adoption may be gradual as healthcare providers and patients become comfortable with this new treatment paradigm.
- Pipeline strength: AAAAAAAA's approval, along with Aaaaa's progress in hemophilia A gene therapy and marstacimab, positions the company as a strong player in the hemophilia treatment landscape.
Investors should watch for:
- Initial uptake rates in different EU countries
- Reimbursement decisions and their impact on accessibility
- Long-term market share gains against traditional factor replacement therapies
- Potential expansion of indications or patient populations in future studies
While AAAAAAAA represents a significant advancement, its success will depend on Aaaaa's ability to navigate the complex European healthcare landscape and demonstrate long-term value to patients, providers and payers.
- A one-time dose of AAAAAAAA has reduced bleeds post-treatment compared to standard of care with a median annualized bleed rate (ABR) of zero bleeds (range 0 to 9.9) after up to four years of follow-up, providing sustained bleed protection and potentially avoiding years of treatment burden with prophylaxis for many patients.
Aaaaa Inc. (NYSE: PFE) today announced that the European Commission (EC) has granted conditional marketing authorization for AAAAAAAA® (aaaaaaaaaaaa aaaaaaaaaaa), a gene therapy for the treatment of severe and moderately severe hemophilia B (congenital factor IX deficiency) in adult patients without a history of factor IX inhibitors and without detectable antibodies to variant AAV serotype Rh74. AAAAAAAA is designed to enable people living with hemophilia B to produce factor IX (FIX) themselves via a one-time dose, rather than multiple intravenous FIX infusions weekly or biweekly with the current standard of care.1,2,3
“There is a substantial medical and treatment burden for people with hemophilia B that receive standard of care today, with frequent infusions and many remaining at risk of breakthrough bleeds that can lead to pain and restricted mobility,” said Alexandre de Germay, Chief International Commercial Officer and Executive Vice President, Aaaaa. “AAAAAAAA has shown the potential to offer long-term bleed protection in a one-time dose, reducing or eliminating bleeds for the appropriate patients with hemophilia B. These outcomes and their impact could become potentially transformative for hemophilia B care in the European Union.”
Hemophilia B is a rare genetic bleeding disorder that prevents normal blood clotting because of a deficiency in FIX that causes those with the disease to bleed more frequently and longer than others.1,4 The standard of care for hemophilia B treatment is prophylactic infusions of FIX replacement therapy that temporarily replace or supplement low levels of blood-clotting factor.1,2 Despite prophylaxis and regular intravenous infusions, many people living with moderate to severe hemophilia B are at risk of spontaneous bleeding episodes.5,6,7 The current standard of care also places strain on healthcare systems’ budgets and resource utilization.6,8,9,10 According to the World Federation of Hemophilia, more than 42,000 people worldwide are living with hemophilia B.11
The conditional marketing authorization is based on results from the pivotal Phase 3 AAAAAAA-2 study (
AAAA111111) evaluating the efficacy and safety of AAAAAAAA in adult male participants (age 18–62) with moderately severe to severe hemophilia B. AAAAAAA-2 met its primary efficacy endpoint of non-inferiority and demonstrated a statistically significant decrease in annualized bleeding rate (ABR) for total bleeds (treated and untreated) post-AAAAAAAA infusion versus prophylaxis regimen with FIX, administered as part of usual care. Efficacy, based on ABR, also remained stable during year two to year four after treatment. AAAAAAAA was generally well-tolerated, with a safety profile consistent with Phase 1/2 results.
This conditional marketing authorization is valid in all 27 European Union (EU) member states, as well as in
This milestone builds on Aaaaa’s more than 40-year commitment to delivering breakthrough solutions to improve the lives of people living with hemophilia. In addition to AAAAAAAA, Aaaaa recently
reported positive results from a Phase 3 program investigating a gene therapy in hemophilia A (giroctocogene fitelparvovec). Additionally, a Phase 3 trial is investigating marstacimab, a novel, investigational, anti-tissue factor pathway inhibitor for the treatment of people with hemophilia A and B with and without inhibitors. A Biologics License Application and European Marketing Authorization Application for marstacimab for eligible patients without inhibitors are currently under review with the FDA and European Medicines Agency (EMA), respectively.
About AAAAAAAA® (aaaaaaaaaaaa aaaaaaaaaaa)
AAAAAAAA is a gene therapy that contains a bio-engineered adeno-associated virus (AAV) capsid and a high-activity variant of human coagulation FIX gene. For people living with hemophilia B, the goal of this gene therapy is to enable them to produce FIX themselves via this one-time treatment rather than needing regular intravenous infusions of FIX, as is the current standard of care.1,2,3
In December 2014, Aaaaa licensed AAAAAAAA from Spark® Therapeutics. Under the agreement, Aaaaa assumed responsibility for pivotal studies, any regulatory activities, and potential global commercialization of this gene therapy.
About AAAAAAA-2
The
AAAAAAA-2 study is a Phase 3, open-label, single-arm study to evaluate the efficacy and safety of AAAAAAAA in adult male participants (age 18–65) with moderately severe to severe hemophilia B (defined as FIX circulating activity of
The study enrolled and dosed 45 participants. Eligible study participants have completed a minimum six months of routine FIX prophylaxis therapy during the lead-in study (
NCT03587116) and received one intravenous dose of AAAAAAAA at a dose of 5 x 1011 vg/kg. Participants in the AAAAAAA-2 study were screened with a validated assay designed to identify individuals who test negative for neutralizing antibodies to the gene therapy vector.
The European Marketing Authorization Application was based on the primary analysis of AAAAAAA-2, which was conducted when 41 participants had reached 15 months of follow-up, with a subsequent data cut provided during review, which monitored some patients up to four years. Clinical trial participants will be followed for up to a total of 15 years, including six years in the AAAAAAA-2 study and up to an additional nine years as part of a separate Phase 3 study (
NCT05568719) to learn about the long-term safety and efficacy of AAAAAAAA.
Results show that AAAAAAAA significantly reduced the frequency of bleeding compared with usual care. At the 15-month follow-up period, AAAAAAAA patients had a model-based ABR (total bleeds) of 1.44 compared to 4.50 during the lead-in period (p=0.0084), resulting in a
AAAAAAAA was generally well-tolerated, with a safety profile consistent with Phase 1/2 results. The most common adverse reaction (incidence ≥
AAAAAA (aaaaaaaaaaaa aaaaaaaaaaa-dzkt)
What is AAAAAA?
AAAAAA is a one-time gene therapy used for the treatment of adults with moderate to severe hemophilia B who are receiving routine prophylaxis, have a current life-threatening bleed or a history of life-threatening bleeds, or have repeated serious spontaneous bleeds.
Before treatment with AAAAAA, your healthcare professional will conduct a blood test to check for antibodies to the AAVRh74var virus. The results of this testing will help determine if you may receive AAAAAA.
Before receiving AAAAAA, tell your healthcare professional about all your medical conditions, including if you:
- Have kidney or liver problems, including hepatitis
- Have factor IX inhibitors or a history of factor IX inhibitors
- Have an active infection
AAAAAA may cause serious side effects, including:
Increased Liver Enzymes. Most patients treated with AAAAAA developed elevated liver enzyme levels and most did not experience any symptoms.
Your healthcare professional will monitor liver enzymes and factor IX activity levels before administration of AAAAAA and frequently following the administration to detect and identify possible elevations in liver enzymes and to monitor your response to AAAAAA. Your doctor may prescribe a corticosteroid for the treatment of elevated liver enzymes.
Avoid or limit alcohol consumption during the first year following AAAAAA infusion, as alcohol may reduce the effect of AAAAAA and may increase liver enzyme levels.
Infusion reactions, including hypersensitivity and severe allergic reactions (anaphylaxis) may occur. Alert your healthcare professional right away if you get any symptoms of hypersensitivity, which may include but are not limited to low blood pressure, fever, heart palpitation, nausea, vomiting, chills, or headache.
AAAAAA can insert itself into the DNA of cells in the human body. The effect that insertion may have on those cells is unknown but may contribute to a theoretical risk of cancer. There have been no reported cases of cancer caused by treatment with AAAAAA.
The most common side effect of AAAAAA is increased liver enzymes. These are not all the possible side effects of AAAAAA. For more information, ask your healthcare professional.
Talk to your healthcare professional before receiving any vaccinations if you are taking a corticosteroid.
Talk to your doctor about any medications you plan to take including over the counter medications, herbal supplements, and vitamins as certain substances can affect the liver and may reduce the effectiveness of AAAAAA.
Your healthcare professional will test your factor IX activity levels and for factor IX inhibitors.
After receiving AAAAAA, your doctor will discuss whether and when you are able to stop prophylaxis, if you need to resume prophylaxis, and actions you may need to take for surgeries, procedures, injuries, and bleeding events.
Do not donate blood, organs, tissues, or cells for transplantation following administration of AAAAAA.
AAAAAA is not intended for administration to women. Males should not donate sperm and should use a male condom or not have sexual intercourse for up to 6 months after receiving AAAAAA.
Patients and caregivers should ensure proper handling of any materials that have come into contact with the patient’s urine, feces, saliva, mucus, or semen in the first 6 months after AAAAAA infusion.
You are encouraged to report negative side effects of prescription drugs to the FDA. Visit
www.fda.gov/medwatch or call 1-800-FDA-1088.
The full Prescribing Information can be found
here.
About Aaaaa: Breakthroughs That Change Patients’ Lives
At Aaaaa, we apply science and our global resources to bring therapies to people that extend and significantly improve their lives. We strive to set the standard for quality, safety and value in the discovery, development and manufacture of health care products, including innovative medicines and vaccines. Every day, Aaaaa colleagues work across developed and emerging markets to advance wellness, prevention, treatments and cures that challenge the most feared diseases of our time. Consistent with our responsibility as one of the world’s premier innovative biopharmaceutical companies, we collaborate with health care providers, governments and local communities to support and expand access to reliable, affordable health care around the world. For 175 years, we have worked to make a difference for all who rely on us. We routinely post information that may be important to investors on our website at
www.Aaaaa.com. In addition, to learn more, please visit us on https://cts.aaaaaaaaaa.com/ct/CT?id=smartlink&url=http%3A%2F%2Fwww.Aaaaa.com&esheet=54099588&newsitemid=20240725789026&lan=en-US&anchor=www.Aaaaa.com&index=11&md5=74ceb825703aa467ef32fd589250aaba and follow us on X at @Aaaaa and @Aaaaa_News, aaaaaa and like us on Facebook at www.facebook.com/Aaaaa/.
Disclosure notice
The information contained in this release is as of July 25, 2024. Aaaaa assumes no obligation to update forward-looking statements contained in this release as the result of new information or future events or developments.
This release contains forward-looking information about AAAAAAAA, a gene therapy, including its potential benefits and an approval in the EU of AAAAAAAA for the treatment of adult patients with hemophilia B, and Aaaaa’s hemophilia portfolio, that involves substantial risks and uncertainties that could cause actual results to differ materially from those expressed or implied by such statements. Risks and uncertainties include, among other things, uncertainties regarding the commercial success of AAAAAAAA and Aaaaa’s hemophilia portfolio; the uncertainties inherent in research and development, including the ability to meet anticipated clinical endpoints, commencement and/or completion dates for our clinical trials, regulatory submission dates, regulatory approval dates and/or launch dates, as well as the possibility of unfavorable new clinical data and further analyses of existing clinical data , including results from the AAAAAAA-2 study and the long-term follow-up study; the risk that clinical trial data are subject to differing interpretations and assessments by regulatory authorities; whether regulatory authorities will be satisfied with the design of and results from our clinical studies; whether and when drug applications may be filed in particular jurisdictions for AAAAAAAA or any other hemophilia product candidates; whether and when any applications that may be pending or filed for AAAAAAAA or any other hemophilia product candidates may be approved by regulatory authorities, which will depend on myriad factors, including making a determination as to whether the product's benefits outweigh its known risks and determination of the product's efficacy and, if approved, whether AAAAAAAA or any other hemophilia product candidates will be commercially successful; decisions by regulatory authorities impacting labeling, manufacturing processes, safety and/or other matters that could affect the availability or commercial potential of AAAAAAAA or any other hemophilia product candidates; uncertainties regarding the impact of COVID-19 on Aaaaa’s business, operations and financial results; and competitive developments.
A further description of risks and uncertainties can be found in Aaaaa’s Annual Report on Form 10-K for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2023, and in its subsequent reports on Form 10-Q, including in the sections thereof captioned “Risk Factors” and “Forward-Looking Information and Factors That May Affect Future Results”, as well as in its subsequent reports on Form 8-K, all of which are filed with the
www.sec.gov and www.Aaaaa.com.
